data.description:
## Incident Overview

**Scenario Type**: Intermittent CI flake / test timeout
**Source**: PR #16 Script Tests job run 25124678958

### Expected Behavior
test-poetry-graceful-degradation.sh passes in CI within the 90s per-file limit.

### Actual Behavior
On the second CI run of PR #16, the test TIMED OUT (exceeded 90s). Test 7 has a 30s inner timeout guard with a code comment explaining the cold-runner risk, but Test 8 (classify-task.sh with a real ticket ID) has no timeout guard. Collectively on cold CI runners, tests can exceed 90s.

### Evidence of Intermittency
- First CI run on PR #16 (run 25124013208): test-poetry-graceful-degradation.sh PASS (8 pass, 0 fail)
- Second CI run on PR #16 (run 25124678958): TIMEOUT (exceeded 90s)
- Main branch CI (run 25116610217): Script Tests passed

### Root Cause
Test 8 calls classify-task.sh with a real ticket ID without a timeout guard. Test 7 already has a timeout guard (30s) precisely because of cold CI runner risks. Test 8 needs the same treatment.
data.title: [test-poetry-graceful-degradation.sh]: Test 8 lacks timeout guard -> test times out on cold CI runners exceeding 90s limit
